_meta.description: D6 fixtures for mastra / observational-memory. Covers the two suggestion pills (large multi-paragraph messages sized to trip Mastra's Observational Memory token threshold). NOTE: these fixtures drive the ACTOR turn only. The `mastra-observational-memory` activity card is produced by @mastra/memory's OM processor on the run's fullStream (data-om-* chunks) + an out-of-band Observer LLM call — NOT by the mocked chat-completion response. aimock has no lever to force those chunks deterministically, so the e2e asserts only the deterministic subset (page + pills + a completing run). See qa/observational-memory.md and tests/e2e/observational-memory.spec.ts.

fixtures._comment: Pill #1 ("Brief my analytics project"). Distinctive substring "Northwind Insights" scopes to this pill without colliding with feature-parity.json. Actor turn: a concise product-risk summary.

response.content:
Here are the top three product risks for Northwind Insights:

1. Query latency on large date ranges. Slow ClickHouse queries directly threaten your north-star (weekly active dashboards). Mitigation: add pre-aggregated rollup tables for common date grains and cache hot funnel/cohort queries.

2. Onboarding friction driving small-account churn. Heavy setup is pushing out exactly the segment you monetize on volume. Mitigation: ship a guided, template-based funnel builder so a new account sees value in the first session.

3. No alerting when metrics move. Customers can't act on shifts they don't see. Mitigation: bring the roadmap's anomaly detection forward and pair it with simple threshold alerts as a fast first cut.

fixtures._comment: Pill #2 ("Plan a two-week trip"). Distinctive substring "Dolomites" scopes to this pill. Actor turn: a rough itinerary.

response.content:
Here's a rough two-week outline for late September in the Dolomites (with an easy Slovenia add-on):

Days 1-4: Base in Ortisei (Val Gardena). Acclimatize with day hikes, then a 2-day hut-to-hut around the Puez-Odle. Family-run guesthouses are easy to find and many kitchens do vegetarian well.

Days 5-8: Base in Cortina d'Ampezzo. Scenic drives over the passes (Giau, Falzarego) plus the Tre Cime loop. Build in one rest day.

Days 9-11: Base in Bolzano. Lower-intensity walks among the vineyards and a thermal-bath rest day at Merano.

Days 12-14: Cross to Lake Bled, Slovenia. A gentler finish with the Vintgar Gorge walk and lake-town food. Fly home from Ljubljana.

Shellfish is easy to avoid inland, and vegetarian options are strong throughout.
